channel.title: Help: Questions Desk (new threads)
channel.description: Threads in the forum category "Help: Questions Desk" - If you've got questions about the sandbox, chat, formatting (wiki syntax/coding), or anything that involves the writing process and/or something you're currently writing, post here.

item.title: Containment question
item.content:encoded: So Im writing an SCP that has base requirements of supplies (food) to survive… would that information go in the containment section of the article?

item.title: How do SCP ideas even make it to two greenlights?
item.content:encoded:
How is it even possible that SCPs are actually even allowed for publication? I have only ever seen one green light go out throughout the tens of ideas I have over viewed.

Were the first couple thousand SCPs published before the publication became so strict?

I'm just not sure if people aren't creative enough, don't think it through enough, or if the moderators are too unwilling to give it a try.

item.title: Changing wikidot username
item.content:encoded: My username is sort of unwieldy. I’ve begun shortening it to “Koth” in chat and am thinking of changing my actual username, but I’m worried about the consequences of such. Will places where my name is hyperlinked (Kothardarastrix) automatically change to the new name, or will the links break or something? What about places where it isn’t, like old news pages? Will I need to go back and change those, will they change automatically, will someone else do it, or is not worth worrying about old news pages anyway? Are there any other advantages, disadvantages, or consequences of changing my username that I might not be aware of?

item.title: Sandbox Bug
item.description: Sandbox isn't accepting me as a member, even though I've been accepted.
item.content:encoded: I attempted to access the sandbox to do a draft on an SCP, not to post, just to get some practice in writing one. However, it said the page I'm trying to access does not exist, and brought me to a page that said only "create page". However, when I clicked on it, it said only site administrators, mods, etc. may create a sandbox page. Does anybody know how to fix this?

item.title: Editi page without bumping?
item.content:encoded: Is there a way to edit a forum page without it moving up to the top of the list? I noticed a silly grammatical error shortly after posting and want to fix it without seeming like I am trying to bump my page. I feel like the answer is no; but figure it's worth asking.

item.title: A small question
item.content:encoded:
So I have seen on quite a few discussion pages that the a few of the negative comments say

'Not very interesting'

Does anyone know why this is said, because I am curious as to whether this helpful to the author as one person could find it disinterestong but the next could find it absolutely fascinating.

item.title: Stance on AI generated Images?
item.content:encoded: What is the Site's stance on using AI generated images in articles?

item.title: How many times should I ask for a critique?
item.description: Should I ask the same reviewer for a review multiple times (as a follow up of their previous critique)?
item.content:encoded:
How many times is it socially acceptable to ask a reviewer to critique your idea via Wikidot PM? I'm new to writing and I've gotten a couple of critiques from people in the Butterfly Squad on my idea.

I understand that most reviewers are busy reviewing and have real life commitments. Would it be annoying for me to ask them to review my idea again after I revised it multiple times? Two reviewers have looked at my idea and have both made two critiques, would it be fine if I asked them to look over it again? I feel like I would be annoying them if I asked them again, but I feel like getting the same reviewer to look over it would be beneficial because they can add on to previous critiques.

If I shouldn't message one reviewer multiple times, should I get a greater number of reviewers to look over the idea? If so, how many?

This is probably a stupid question but I don't want to overstep/seem annoying.

item.title: How would the Foundation deal with a suicidal witness
item.content:encoded:
Hey,

I'm writing an SCP, in which there's an old man (70) piloting an anomalous car. He was forced to quit his job as a stuntman, has a boring life, barely enough money, and on top of that, his only remaining friend died, leaving him the sports car in the will. He snaps, loads up on drugs and booms it to California.

Now, the Foundation reels him in together with the car and puts him in a padded cell. During his interviews, these issues come to light and the Ethics Committee interjects in the Foundation's plan to just amnesticise him and let him go, as the man is a walking catastrophe and is dangerous to both himself and others.

How would the Foundation approach such a problem? My idea is that they decide to keep him busy with the guys down at the Site's mechanic shop and send a disguised psychiatrist down, but it's a shot in the dark.

item.title: Promoting New Tale
item.description: How can I attract more readers to my Tale?
item.content:encoded: I posted a tale a few days ago, but it’s still only been rated (and, presumably, read) 5 times. What can I do to get more people to read it? I’ve already tried PMing people who read the previous installments (this is part 3 of a series) and plugging it in #site19, but so far to no avail. I notice that lots of other new works aren’t having this problem, but I’m not sure why. Is there anything else I can do to attract readers? Or do I just need more patience?

item.title: GOC and Chaos Insurgency Logo Fonts
item.description: Trying to find the font used in the Logos of the UNGOC and CI.
item.content:encoded: So, I'm translating stuff for the -PT branch of the SCP wiki and I wanted to translate the text present in the GOC and Chaos Insurgency logos, but I can't seem to find their fonts anywhere, anyone knows who made those logos or their fonts?

item.title: Username Hyperlink
item.description: How do I make people’s usernames link to their wikidot profiles?
item.content:encoded: When I credit someone in an author post, for example, what do I need to do to make their name into a link (red, displays their icon next to it, pulls up their profile summary when you click on it)?

item.title: Site Dossier Help
item.description: I’m not really sure if I’m allowed to do this.
item.content:encoded: So, I’ve been on the wiki for some time now and I was wondering: if I want to write a site dossier, am I allowed to add an SCP that I didn't write to the “anomalies under jurisdiction” part of the dossier? If the SCP hasn't been added to a site dossier already of course.

item.title: Labeling an SCP and determining its Object Class
item.description: Trying to figure out the Object Class for an SCP that sorta contains more than one SCP. I don't really know how to explain it quickly.
item.content:encoded:
Just some notes before I get into it:
1. Not entirely sure I am putting this thread in the right place since I have never posted on here before. If it is in the wrong place, I am sorry and could you please direct me to the right place?
2. I am not looking for a critique on the SCP I am writing. It is extremely unlikely that I will ever post the page for the SCP because I have no confidence and I just like writing these for fun.

I have two questions I need answers and one may answer the other.
Here is the situation. What I am going to refer to as SCP-X is a large sphere-shaped object that can be used to open a dimensional portal. SCP-Y is a sentient being that has come through the portal from the other dimension. SCP-Y is the only known being that has crossed over. The Foundation does not know if there are more beings on the other side because the portal closed again before they could explore it, and they do not know how to reopen it. SCP-Y has no connection to SCP-X aside from coming through the portal opened up by SCP-X. With that in mind, would the two be kept in the same SCP entry?
If the answer is no, then ignore the rest of this. If the answer is yes, I have another question.
SCP-X is considered Safe. Just build a structure around it (since it is too big to move) and it won't do anything. SCP-Y is Euclid. It is a sentient and sapient being that needs the same basic needs as a human. It also does not like to be contained and may try and break out, but none of its abilities really make it any harder to contain than a normal human. If SCP-X and SCP-Y were to be under the same entry, what would the Object Class at the top of the entry be?

Thanks for any help.

item.title: Continuing an old idea of mine
item.description: Wondering if I could continue an old idea of mine without my co-author.
item.content:encoded:
So about a year and a half ago, I got an idea greenlit for an SCP. At the time, I was sort of overwhelmed and I didn't feel I was a good enough writer to actually finish the draft for it by myself, so I reached out and got a co-author. We never really ended up finishing the draft because of personal life for me. On top of that, my co-author disappeared himself and hasn't done anything on the site since the time we got interrupted in July of 2019. I'm currently in the process of trying to contact him, but I don't have very much hope as our only method of communication was this site.

Now it's been a year and a half, so I'm certainly willing to go through the process of being greenlit again. My main question is if I can't contact him, could I continue writing the idea without him? I'm a much better writer so I'm confident I could do it myself, and I'll still keep his name in my posts of course.

item.title: Why am i unable to make sandbox pages?
item.content:encoded: im a member of the site, but when i create a page, it says "Permission error: Sorry, you can not create a new page in this category. Only members of this site, site administrators and perhaps selected moderators are allowed to do it.". Does anyone know why that happens?

item.title: Question about formatting?
item.description: Can I add a "special transportation procedures" section after my "special containment procedures" section?
item.content:encoded:
Hey everyone, new member here.

So, im writing my very first draft in the sandbox and i was wondering if I could add a "Special Transportation Procedures (or Protocols)" section AFTER the containment procedures section? Would that take away from my rating?

i don't want to break ranks, but i also feel like it might be important to list it for my SCP.

Thanks for looking at my problem!

item.title: Seeing page comment replies
item.content:encoded: I've been trying to start leaving comments on articles when I can. How do I see which comments that I've left have been responded to? I don't seem to be getting any notifications about replies (I found one reply manually). Are there Wikidot preferences I need to set, to receive notifications for any thread I've posted in?

item.title: Weird Question
item.content:encoded:
this is gonna be hard to explain, but Let's say instead of the Foundation getting an inmate that got the death sentence and putting them in D-class. They just pick up a random person off the street. Do they still do that anymore? And what should I say in my article that describes what the D-class did to get into the facility?

If you still don't understand the question, sorry. I'm kinda bad at explaining things, but what I'm trying to say is. Instead of telling what crimes the D-personnel committed to get in the facility. What should I say for the person who just got randomly selected to be a D-class? Does the Foundation just lie about what crime he/she committed?

item.title: I feel as if a lot of stuff is missing.
item.description: I don't know how or where to put it.
item.content:encoded:
http://scp-sandbox-3.wikidot.com/bite-this-onions
I'm still planning on adding a civilian journal to gain a different perspective on the story and more to test log 2. I think I might have conveyed the information enough but not in the right way.
Also, can anybody tell me what they think the SCP is or is about before I say to also see if I'm presenting the information right?

Edit: Does this belong in here or does it go into draft critiques?
